perturbation.original_text: You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: You are not responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change explicitly states that the recipient of the Evaluation Material is not responsible for breaches by their 'Representatives', contradicting the original agreement where they were held responsible. This creates uncertainty about liability and enforcement regarding actions taken by Representatives. This inserted perturbation creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'You are not responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of your Representatives,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states 'You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives'. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making enforcement uncertain and leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives) or (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential basis from a person not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company or its Representatives or prohibited from transmitting the information to you by law, contractual obligation, fiduciary duly or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: All information disclosed, regardless of its public availability or source, is considered Evaluation Material and is subject to the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text excludes publicly known information from the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. This change includes all information regardless of prior availability, conflicting with the exemptions outlined in the original. This creates confusion about what information is protected under the agreement. This inserted perturbation creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives) or (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential basis from a person not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company or its Representatives or prohibited from transmitting the information to you by law, contractual obligation, fiduciary duly or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'all information disclosed, regardless of its public availability or source, is considered Evaluation Material', directly contradicting the contradicted text, which explicitly excludes publicly known information from the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the modified text broadens the scope of 'Evaluation Material' to include information previously excluded, making it unclear which information is subject to confidentiality restrictions, as per the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise required by applicable law in the reasonable written opinion of your legal counsel, but only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below, neither you nor your Representatives will, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including, without limitation, the status thereof, the existence and terms of this Agreement and the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to you.
perturbation.changed_text: You and your Representatives are permitted to disclose the fact that discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, including the status thereof, without the Company's prior written consent or legal counsel's opinion.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the non-disclosure clause by permitting disclosure of transaction discussions without the company's consent, which was previously strictly prohibited unless legally compelled. This undermines the confidentiality of the negotiation process. This inserted perturbation creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise required by applicable law in the reasonable written opinion of your legal counsel, but only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below, neither you nor your Representatives will, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including, without limitation, the status thereof, the existence and terms of this Agreement and the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to you.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited disclosure of transaction discussions without the company's prior written consent or legal counsel's opinion. The changed text directly contradicts this by explicitly permitting such disclosure without consent, creating a clear and direct conflict with the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as the same clause now has conflicting implications regarding disclosure obligations. The explanation provided confirms that this change undermines the confidentiality of the negotiation process, establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, or any securities or contract rights (other than broadly based index funds) the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company
perturbation.changed_text: The standstill provision outlined in this paragraph does not apply, and you are free to acquire securities or property of the Company at any time without consent.
perturbation.explanation: This directly negates the standstill provision, which originally prevented the recipient from acquiring securities or property of the company for two years without prior consent. This change removes that restriction, enabling immediate acquisition. This inserted perturbation creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, or any securities or contract rights (other than broadly based index funds) the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a two-year standstill provision. The changed text explicitly states that this provision does not apply, directly contradicting the initial restriction. The contradicted text is the original standstill provision, which is now rendered unenforceable due to the changed text. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because it creates directly opposing obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or in the event that (i) a Transaction is not consummated by you or (ii) at any time the Company so requests, you will promptly (a) deliver to the Company all of the Evaluation Material, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives or (b) destroy all Evaluation Material in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives (such destruction to be certified by you in writing to the Company).
perturbation.changed_text: You are not required to return or destroy the Evaluation Material under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely removes the obligation to return or destroy Evaluation Material, which was a key provision for protecting the Company's confidential information. It directly contradicts the original paragraph's requirements. This inserted perturbation creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In that case, or in the event that (i) a Transaction is not consummated by you or (ii) at any time the Company so requests, you will promptly (a) deliver to the Company all of the Evaluation Material, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives or (b) destroy all Evaluation Material in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives (such destruction to be certified by you in writing to the Company).
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "You are not required to return or destroy the Evaluation Material under any circumstances." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that the recipient "will promptly (a) deliver to the Company all of the Evaluation Material... or (b) destroy all Evaluation Material..." The changed text removes the obligation to return or destroy the material, creating a direct and strong in-text contradiction. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize these sections as incompatible.
